metadata.title: Why a Broken Oxygen Sensor Causes a Cancer to Act Permanently Starved
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Biology Candidate 12. Mechanism only: VHL loss causes constitutive HIF-1alpha accumulation regardless of oxygen — the recognition step that commits HIF-1alpha to degradation is absent.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, TEAL = pathway working / HIF destroyed / normal cell outcome; CRIMSON = VHL absent / HIF accumulating / cancer cell outcome; gold highlighter as the single editor's pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = the working degradation pathway / HIF-1alpha destroyed on schedule / normal cell; CRIMSON #BF3339 = VHL absent / HIF-1alpha accumulating constitutively / clear cell RCC; GOLD = single editor-highlight on the missing VHL node; never swapped mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO full prolyl hydroxylase biochemistry beyond naming PHDs; NO HIF-1alpha vs HIF-2alpha distinction beyond the single belzutifan mention in the endcard; NO oncogenic pseudohypoxia routes (SDH mutation, KRAS/MYC); NO VEGF/angiogenesis mechanism detail beyond naming VEGF as an output. Manim move: compare (left panel normal, right panel VHL-null). The worked example (HIF-1alpha half-life) appears right before RECAP as required by SLATE-RUNNER act structure.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Clear cell kidney cancer runs a full hypoxic-adaptation program — in tumor regions with adequate oxygen.

card.copy: Why a broken oxygen sensor causes a cancer to act permanently starved

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] broken oxygen sensor — permanent starvation

beats.narration_text: A clear cell renal tumor biopsy shows high GLUT1, high VEGF, full hypoxic-adaptation program running. Measure oxygen in that region: partial pressure adequate. Normal. The cells behave starved. The oxygen is present.

props.headline: A biopsy of a clear cell renal tumor shows high GLUT1,…

beats.scene_description: a cross-section photomicrograph of clear cell RCC tissue — pale lipid-loaded cells under the microscope, overlaid with teal annotation chips reading GLUT1 and VEGF; slow push-in toward the pale cytoplasm
beats.image_prompt: B02, cross-section histology photomicrograph of clear cell renal cell carcinoma tissue, pale vacuolated cytoplasm from lipid droplets, grid of clear cells under microscope, teal annotation labels reading GLUT1 and VEGF pinned to the image, desaturated newsprint editorial treatment, flat print reproduction
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] clear cell RCC histology — teal GLUT1/VEGF labels on the pale cells

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL ai] clear cell RCC histology — teal GLUT1/VEGF labels on the pale cells

beats.narration_text: HIF-1 alpha drives the hypoxic program. It accumulates when oxygen falls, then drives transcription of glucose transporters, glycolytic enzymes, and VEGF. In a well-oxygenated cell, HIF-1 alpha should be essentially undetectable.

production_viz.label: HIF-1a drives the hypoxic program
production_viz.mechanic: a central chip labeled HIF-1alpha in TEAL expands on screen; three arrows fan out right toward three output chips: GLUT1, glycolytic enzymes, VEGF in TEAL; a small eyebrow serif label reads 'hypoxic response master switch'
production_viz.note: establish HIF-1alpha as the one pivot — three outputs, one driver

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] HIF-1alpha as hub: three arrows to GLUT1, glycolytic enzymes, VEGF

beats.narration_text: The gap: the HIF-1 alpha gene is intact in clear cell kidney cancer. The oxygen sensors — prolyl hydroxylases — are intact. Oxygen is adequate. Yet HIF-1 alpha is constitutively active. Which node in the pathway is broken?

card.copy: HIF-1alpha gene: intact. Prolyl hydroxylases: intact. Oxygen: adequate. HIF-1alpha: constitutively active.
card.dek: Why does a cell with normal oxygen-sensing fire the hypoxic program anyway?

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD question] the gap — every upstream node is fine, yet HIF is on

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD question] the gap — every upstream node is fine, yet HIF is on

beats.narration_text: Expected pathway: oxygen sensor intact, oxygen adequate — HIF-1 alpha hydroxylated and destroyed on schedule. The sensing step fires. The destruction step completes. The cells are not hypoxic.

production_viz.label: expected: sensor works, HIF destroyed
production_viz.mechanic: a simple linear chain, left to right: O2 chip -> PHD chip (labeled 'hydroxylates HIF-1a') -> expected outcome chip in TEAL labeled 'HIF-1a destroyed'; arrows between each step drawn on in TEAL; the whole chain sits in a TEAL-bordered panel labeled 'expected in a normoxic cell'
production_viz.note: naive expectation: full pathway = HIF gone. Sets up the mystery.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] expected normoxic chain: O2 -> PHD hydroxylates -> HIF destroyed

beats.narration_text: The break is not at oxygen. Not at the hydroxylase. The failure is one step downstream: the protein that reads the hydroxylation tag. VHL.
beats.new_visual_element: [CARD section] THE MECHANISM — what VHL does

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD section] THE MECHANISM — what VHL does

beats.narration_text: In a normoxic cell: prolyl hydroxylases add a hydroxyl group to two proline residues on HIF-1 alpha. That tag is the signal. VHL reads it — binds the hydroxylated HIF-1 alpha, recruits a ubiquitin ligase, commits the protein to proteasomal destruction. HIF-1 alpha half-life: roughly five minutes. Made and immediately gone.

production_viz.label: normal pathway: hydroxylation -> VHL recognition -> destruction
production_viz.mechanic: left-to-right chain: PHD chip (TEAL) adds hydroxyl mark to HIF-1a block, then VHL chip (TEAL) binds it, then proteasome destroys it; the HIF-1a block shrinks to nothing at the right; serif label '~5 min half-life' below the chain; TEAL throughout — this is the working pathway
production_viz.note: the canonical pathway — make VHL's recognition step visually prominent so its absence lands in B09

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] normal degradation chain: PHD hydroxylates -> VHL binds -> proteasome destroys

beats.narration_text: Clear cell kidney cancer loses VHL — through deletion, mutation, or methylation — in nearly all cases. Without VHL, HIF-1 alpha is hydroxylated on schedule. The tag is there. No reader. Destruction machinery never recruited. HIF-1 alpha accumulates.

production_viz.label: VHL absent: HIF-1a hydroxylated but never destroyed
production_viz.mechanic: same chain layout as B07 but now: PHD chip (TEAL) still hydroxylates HIF-1a — the mark is placed; the VHL node is missing — shown as a CRIMSON dashed outline with serif label 'VHL absent'; HIF-1a block with its hydroxyl mark drifts rightward accumulating — CRIMSON pile building up; proteasome chip present but disconnected; GOLD highlighter bar across the empty VHL node
production_viz.note: the mechanism reveal — PHD still works, VHL is the hole, HIF piles up. GOLD used once here on the missing VHL node.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] VHL absent: PHD works, VHL node is missing (GOLD highlight), HIF-1a accumulates (CRIMSON)

beats.narration_text: Result: constitutive HIF-1 alpha activity — not because the cell is hypoxic, but because the off-switch is missing. Sensing completed. Disposal absent. HIF-1 alpha floods the nucleus and runs the full hypoxic transcriptional program in a normoxic cell.

production_viz.label: HIF-1a floods nucleus: full hypoxic program in a normoxic cell
production_viz.mechanic: the accumulated CRIMSON HIF-1a block from B08 moves left into a nucleus oval (large circle, CRIMSON border); three output arrows fan right: GLUT1, glycolytic enzymes, VEGF in CRIMSON; a serif annotation reads 'well-oxygenated cell'; the paradox is on screen — CRIMSON program firing in a normoxic cell
production_viz.note: CRIMSON throughout — this is the broken outcome. Mirror of B03's structure for contrast.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] HIF floods nucleus (CRIMSON): GLUT1, glycolytic enzymes, VEGF in a normoxic cell

beats.narration_text: Side by side. Left: normal cell — oxygen adequate, PHDs active, VHL present. HIF-1 alpha hydroxylated, recognized, destroyed. Functionally absent. Right: VHL-null clear cell RCC — same oxygen, same PHDs working, VHL absent. HIF-1 alpha hydroxylated and never destroyed. Constitutively active.

production_viz.label: the compare: normal cell vs VHL-null RCC
production_viz.mechanic: split screen, left panel TEAL-bordered labeled 'normal cell', right panel CRIMSON-bordered labeled 'clear cell RCC'. Left: PHD -> VHL -> proteasome -> HIF gone (TEAL chain). Right: PHD working, VHL node missing (CRIMSON dashed), HIF accumulating (CRIMSON stack). Dividing line center. Serif label on each panel. The manim move: compare.
production_viz.note: the film's core graphic — the compare move. Two identical upstream steps, one divergent downstream node.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC compare] left: normal pathway TEAL, right: VHL-null accumulation CRIMSON

beats.narration_text: The cancer is not detecting low oxygen. It has lost the protein that reads the oxygen signal and acts on it. The sensor reports correctly. The report goes nowhere.

card.sub: what VHL loss means for the tumor

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD section] THE IMPLICATION — VHL loss drives the tumor phenotype

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD section] THE IMPLICATION — VHL loss drives the tumor phenotype

beats.narration_text: Consequence: full hypoxic adaptation running at full throttle in a well-oxygenated tumor. GLUT1 upregulated — glucose floods in. Glycolytic enzymes upregulated — carbon routed to lactate and building blocks. VEGF secreted — new vessels recruited. Warburg metabolism, clear lipid-loaded cytoplasm, high vascularity — all downstream of one missing protein.

props.headline: The consequence is the entire hypoxic adaptation program running at full…

beats.scene_description: a schematic diagram showing the downstream consequences of VHL loss: a central VHL-absent node in crimson, with three consequence arrows pointing to GLUT1 (glucose uptake), glycolytic enzymes (Warburg metabolism), and VEGF (angiogenesis), each in teal on a newsprint editorial background
beats.image_prompt: B12, editorial schematic diagram on aged newsprint, central node labeled VHL ABSENT in crimson bold serif, three diverging arrows pointing to labeled outcome boxes: GLUT1 high (glucose uptake), glycolytic enzymes high (Warburg metabolism), VEGF high (angiogenesis), arrows in teal, flat print reproduction desaturated editorial collage
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] editorial schematic — VHL absent node, three consequence arrows

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [STILL ai] editorial schematic — VHL absent node, three consequence arrows

beats.narration_text: Worked example. Normal kidney cell: HIF-1 alpha synthesized continuously. Prolyl hydroxylases tag it in about 45 seconds. VHL binds in about 10 seconds. Proteasome destroys it. Net half-life: roughly 5 minutes. Steady-state level: essentially undetectable.

production_viz.label: normal cell: HIF-1a synthesized and destroyed, half-life ~5 min
production_viz.mechanic: a horizontal timeline, left to right: synthesis arrow -> HIF-1a block appears -> PHD tag added (45 sec label) -> VHL binds (10 sec label) -> proteasome destroys -> HIF-1a gone. All in TEAL. A serif label 'half-life ~5 min' under the timeline. A small bar at far right reading 'steady state: undetectable'.
production_viz.note: illustrative timing from the card — 45 sec PHD, 10 sec VHL, ~5 min half-life. Labeled illustrative.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] normal cell timeline: synthesis -> PHD 45s -> VHL 10s -> destroyed, half-life ~5 min

beats.narration_text: VHL-null clear cell RCC: same synthesis rate. Same prolyl hydroxylase activity — HIF-1 alpha hydroxylated on schedule. VHL absent. Hydroxylated protein accumulates. After four hours: roughly 40 times normal levels. GLUT1 rises. VEGF secreted. Glycolytic enzymes upregulated. Detection step completed. Disposal step missing.

production_viz.label: VHL-null RCC: same synthesis, same PHD — HIF-1a accumulates to 40x at 4 hours
production_viz.mechanic: same layout as B13: synthesis -> PHD tags (45 sec, TEAL) -> VHL node: CRIMSON dashed box labeled 'VHL absent' — HIF-1a block does NOT disappear but instead stacks up on the right side in CRIMSON. A CRIMSON stacked pile grows. A serif label 'after 4 hours: ~40x normal (illustrative)' with GOLD highlight bar below the stacked pile. Small output chips: GLUT1 up, VEGF secreted, glycolytic enzymes up, all in CRIMSON.
production_viz.note: illustrative numbers per card: 40x at 4 hours. PHD step TEAL (it works), VHL CRIMSON (absent), accumulation CRIMSON. GOLD highlight bar once on the 40x label — do not double-use GOLD from B08; this is a different scene.

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] VHL-null timeline: PHD works (TEAL), VHL absent (CRIMSON), 40x accumulation, GLUT1/VEGF/enzymes up

beats.narration_text: VHL reads the hydroxylation tag and commits HIF-1 alpha to degradation. Lose VHL and HIF-1 alpha accumulates constitutively regardless of oxygen — running the entire hypoxic-adaptation program in a cell with adequate oxygen. A broken off-switch, not a false alarm.

card.copy: VHL reads the hydroxylation tag and commits HIF-1alpha to destruction.
card.dek: Lose VHL and HIF-1alpha accumulates regardless of oxygen — the off-switch is missing, not the alarm.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD recap] VHL = reader of the tag; absent -> constitutive HIF regardless of O2

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD recap] VHL = reader of the tag; absent -> constitutive HIF regardless of O2
